examples.title: Grid Search for Hyperparameter Optimization
examples.description: A pseudocode implementation of grid search for hyperparameter optimization.
examples.code:
FOR each hyperparameter combination
  TRAIN the model using the current combination
  EVALUATE the performance of the model
  IF the performance is better than the previous best
    UPDATE the best combination and performance
END FOR
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implementation of grid search iterates over each possible combination of hyperparameters, trains the model using the current combination, evaluates the performance of the model, and updates the best combination and performance if the current combination results in better performance.

examples.title: Random Search for Hyperparameter Optimization
examples.description: A Python implementation of random search for hyperparameter optimization using the scikit-learn library.
examples.code:
from sklearn.model_selection import RandomizedSearchCV
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

# Define the hyperparameter space
param_grid = {
  'n_estimators': [10, 50, 100],
  'max_depth': [None, 5, 10]
}

# Initialize the random search object
random_search = RandomizedSearchCV(RandomForestClassifier(), param_grid, cv=5, n_iter=10)

# Fit the random search object to the data
random_search.fit(X, y)
examples.explanation: This Python implementation of random search uses the scikit-learn library to define the hyperparameter space, initialize the random search object, and fit the random search object to the data.
